narrative:
Mike Maignan is the starting goalkeeper for France. His overall rating of 7.13/10 places him firmly among the top 9% of goalkeepers at this tournament. This rating primarily reflects his consistent club form over the last two seasons, contributing 90% to his current score.

Maignan has played 450 minutes at this World Cup, demonstrating his consistent presence. He averages 1.6 saves per 90 minutes, highlighting his shot-stopping ability. His minimal expected assists of 0.001 per 90 minutes at this World Cup indicates he is not a distribution-focused goalkeeper.

Maignan is a crucial, reliable presence in goal for the French national team. Scouts should continue to observe his shot-stopping consistency, given his 1.6 saves per 90 minutes. Fans can expect him to maintain his primary role as a pure goalkeeper, rather than a deep-lying playmaker.
